entries.feed_content: Welcome — this is a read-only site powered by pglite-feed. Content is managed externally via content.json and refreshes automatically when updated.
Everything runs in your browser via PGlite (Postgres in WebAssembly). Nothing is sent to a server — each visitor gets their own private copy of the data.
Use the search bar to find entries. Multi-word AND matching, -exclusion, after: and before: date filtering all work.
Type # in the search bar to browse all hashtag categories as a cloud. Click any tag to filter. The × button clears the search.
Content supports [markdown links](https://example.com) and bare URLs like https://example.com — both become clickable.
Read more [about this app](public/about.md) — rendered inline with the retro theme.
To create your own site: fork [pglite-feed on GitHub](https://github.com/robertvigil/pglite-feed), edit content.json, and deploy.
Set the title and theme in the config section of content.json. Available themes: green (default), amber, white.
Tagged entries are hidden from the default view. Type # to browse them. #demo
These are sample entries from content.example.json. Replace them with your own content. #demo
